old_claim.note: Basic Geometry and Surveying is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: A marked cubit rod embodies a linear standard; formal geometry and surveying are applications, not prerequisites for the artifact.

source_shape.source_locator: Object record: identifies a wooden Egyptian cubit measuring rod from Dynasties 12-13, dated about 1981-1640 BCE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Met documents a surviving wooden Egyptian cubit rod dated about 1981-1640 BCE.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. A marked cubit rod embodies a linear standard; formal geometry and surveying are applications, not prerequisites for the artifact.

ontology_before: measuring_rods_and_lines treated basic_geometry_and_surveying as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: measuring_rods_and_lines is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims basic_geometry_and_surveying as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: measuring_rods_and_lines no longer directly depends on basic_geometry_and_surveying.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current measuring_rods_and_lines scope technically requires basic_geometry_and_surveying.
short_reason: A marked cubit rod embodies a linear standard; formal geometry and surveying are applications, not prerequisites for the artifact.
